era_context: The final year of Emperor Hadrian's reign in the Roman Empire. Hadrian, aged 61, is in declining health and will die in July 138 CE. In China, the Eastern Han Dynasty continues its gradual institutional decline. The Parthian Empire remains dominant in the Near East and Central Asia. This year marks the end of an era of significant Roman military expansion and architectural ambition.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most regions outside the Mediterranean and China

events.title: Hadrian's health declines; succession arrangements finalized
events.description: In his final year of life, Emperor Hadrian, aged 61, is in declining health. He finalizes the succession by formally adopting Antoninus Pius as his heir, ensuring a smooth transition of power. This decision reflects Hadrian's concern for the stability of the empire after his death. Hadrian spends much of this year at his villa in Tivoli, away from Rome. The adoption of Antoninus Pius is a significant political act that will determine the course of the empire for the next 23 years.
events.certainty_note: Hadrian's adoption of Antoninus Pius is well-attested, though the exact timing within 137–138 CE is not precisely documented. The adoption likely occurred in 138 CE, shortly before Hadrian's death in July.

events.title: No other major documented events for this year
events.description: 137 CE is not marked by any other major documented historical events in surviving sources. The year falls within the broader era of late Hadrianic rule and Eastern Han decline, but no specific events of historical significance beyond Hadrian's health decline are recorded in available sources.
events.certainty_note: The absence of documented events is consistent with the sparse documentation of the 2nd century CE.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. The sparse documentation for 137 CE reflects the general pattern of 2nd-century sources.
historiographic_note: 137 CE is poorly documented in surviving sources. The year is known primarily for Hadrian's declining health and the finalization of succession arrangements, but specific events are not detailed in available sources. The Historia Augusta is the primary source but is known to contain fabrications. Chinese sources are similarly silent on specific events for 137 CE. The year represents a transitional period between Hadrian's reign and Antoninus Pius's, but the transition itself is not documented in detail until 138 CE.

graph_edges.note: Hadrian's declining health and succession arrangements lead to Antoninus Pius's accession in 138 CE
